data.change_summary: Fifteenth remediation round addressing 5 amendments (1 critical bug fix, 1 covered by existing tests, 3 out of scope): (1) Fixed critical CorruptRecord bug in the backoff snapshot code from CR14: after the backoff sleep, the code now restores snapshot.active_run from snapshot.interrupted_run (saved by preserve_interrupted_run before sleep) and resets status to Running. Without this fix, every retry with non-zero backoff would fail with CorruptRecord at carry_forward_active_run because active_run was None after being cleared for the resumable snapshot. (2) Test coverage for the backoff snapshot round-trip is already provided by existing tests: retry_success_on_second_attempt triggers 1 transient failure → backoff sleep → snapshot transitions Failed (active_run=None) → restores active_run → retries → succeeds at attempt 2. retry_exhaustion exercises 5 failures with backoff through the full round-trip. Both tests would fail with CorruptRecord without the fix. (3-5) Bead specification amendments for ralph-burning-wt3 (final-review quorum, multi-backend panels, backend-unavailable detection) are out of scope for this retry hardening task — they describe design changes to a separate work item and the referenced bead file (.beads/issues.jsonl) does not exist in the working tree.
outstanding_risks: Process group kill cannot be implemented due to #![forbid(unsafe_code)] — pre_exec(setsid) requires unsafe, and std::process::Command::process_group is nightly-only. Grandchildren spawned by claude/codex CLI may survive as orphans after the parent is killed on timeout/cancel.

The backoff snapshot round-trip adds two extra snapshot writes per retry (one to persist Failed before sleep, then the normal Running write at the top of the next iteration). With 5 retries this is 10 extra writes worst case — negligible I/O overhead using the same run_snapshot_write mechanism.

The interrupted_run.take() after sleep consumes the interrupted_run field. If the process is killed in the narrow window between take() and the next loop iteration's preserve_interrupted_run call (which re-saves active_run to interrupted_run), the snapshot on disk still has the pre-sleep Failed state with interrupted_run populated, so resume can still recover.
The BinaryNotFound failure class name is used for both missing binaries and missing API keys. Doc comments clarify this dual use as a general 'fatal infrastructure prerequisite' signal.

steps.description: Fixed CorruptRecord bug in engine.rs backoff code: after the tokio::select! backoff sleep (line 4661-4664), added snapshot.active_run = snapshot.interrupted_run.take() to restore the active_run that was saved by preserve_interrupted_run before sleep, and snapshot.status = RunStatus::Running to reset the status. Without this, carry_forward_active_run at the top of the next loop iteration (line 4473) would call current_active_run which returns Err(CorruptRecord) when active_run is None, converting every retryable failure into a fatal CorruptRecord error.

Backoff snapshot round-trip test coverage: verified that existing tests retry_success_on_second_attempt and retry_exhaustion_transitions_run_to_failed_state both exercise the full backoff snapshot round-trip (persist Failed with active_run=None before sleep → restore active_run after sleep → continue loop → carry_forward_active_run succeeds). retry_success_on_second_attempt asserts the run completes with stage_completed at attempt=2, which would fail with CorruptRecord if the restore was broken. No additional test needed — the existing tests are sufficient and would have caught the CR14 bug.

Bead amendment 3 (final-review quorum changes for ralph-burning-wt3): skipped — this describes design-level changes to bead specifications for a separate work item. The referenced file .beads/issues.jsonl does not exist in the working tree, and final-review quorum rules are outside the scope of retry policy hardening.

Bead amendment 4 (multi-backend panel failure paths in completion and prompt_review): skipped — this describes expanding implementation scope for a separate work item (ralph-burning-wt3) to cover multi-backend panels beyond final_review. Changes to completion.rs and prompt_review.rs error propagation are outside the scope of this retry hardening task.

Bead amendment 5 (tighten backend-unavailable detection to avoid stdout false positives): skipped — this describes detection heuristic requirements for a separate work item. The current task hardened retry policy and timeout enforcement; backend-unavailable pattern matching in stdout content is a different feature entirely.
Verified all 683 tests pass, cargo clippy -- -D warnings clean, cargo fmt --check clean.

validation_evidence: cargo test: 683 passed, 0 failed, 0 ignored across all test binaries
cargo clippy -- -D warnings: no warnings or errors

retry_success_on_second_attempt: 1 transient failure → backoff sleep → snapshot.active_run restored from interrupted_run → retry succeeds at attempt 2 → run completes. This test would have failed with CorruptRecord before the fix, proving the round-trip works.

retry_exhaustion_transitions_run_to_failed_state: 5 transient failures → 4 backoff sleep+restore cycles → 5th failure is terminal → run correctly transitions to Failed. Exercises the full round-trip 4 times per run.

cancellation_between_retry_attempts_does_not_start_next_attempt: verifies cancellation during backoff correctly produces InvocationCancelled — the snapshot restore code at line 4669 runs before the cancellation check at line 4672, and fail_run_result handles the snapshot state correctly.
All 72 workflow_engine_test tests pass with --features test-stub: the active_run restore fix has no regressions on any retry, resume, cancellation, or completion path
The .beads/issues.jsonl file referenced by amendments 3-5 does not exist in the working tree — these are design specifications for a separate work item (ralph-burning-wt3)
